title: Identify, Report, and Correct System Flaws
description: Identify, report, and correct information system flaws in a timely manner.

checks.name: Windows Update is Enabled and Configured
checks.purpose: Verify the Windows Update service is running and configured to receive and install updates

checks.name: No Critical or High Patches are Missing
checks.purpose: Confirm no critical or high severity patches are missing from the system based on the last update scan

checks.name: Last Successful Windows Update is Within Policy Window
checks.purpose: Verify the system has successfully applied updates within the defined patching window (typically 30 days for critical, 90 days for high)

checks.name: Patch Management Agent is Active
checks.purpose: Confirm a patch management agent (WSUS client, SCCM, Intune, or equivalent) is installed and communicating with the patch management server

checks.name: Windows Update is Enabled and Configured
checks.purpose: Verify the Windows Update service is running and configured to receive and install updates on the server

checks.name: No Critical or High Patches are Missing
checks.purpose: Confirm no critical or high severity patches are missing from the server based on the last update scan

checks.name: Last Successful Windows Update is Within Policy Window
checks.purpose: Verify the server has successfully applied updates within the defined patching window

checks.name: Patch Management Agent is Active
checks.purpose: Confirm a patch management agent is installed and communicating with the patch management server

checks.name: Pending Reboot for Patch Completion is Detected
checks.purpose: Detect if a pending reboot is blocking patch completion, which would leave the system in a partially patched and potentially vulnerable state

checks.name: Package Manager Has Access to Security Update Repositories
checks.purpose: Verify security update repositories are configured and reachable in /etc/yum.repos.d/ or /etc/apt/sources.list

checks.name: No Critical Security Packages are Pending Update
checks.purpose: Confirm no outstanding security updates are available by checking the package manager update cache

checks.name: Last Package Update is Within Policy Window
checks.purpose: Verify the system has applied security updates within the defined patching window by checking package manager logs

checks.name: Kernel is Current and Not Vulnerable
checks.purpose: Check that the running kernel version is the latest available and no kernel security updates are pending

checks.name: APT Security Update Repositories are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify Debian security repositories are present and reachable in /etc/apt/sources.list or /etc/apt/sources.list.d/

checks.name: No Critical Security Packages are Pending Update
checks.purpose: Confirm no outstanding security updates are available via apt-get --dry-run upgrade or apt list --upgradable

checks.name: Last Package Update is Within Policy Window
checks.purpose: Verify the system has applied security updates within the defined patching window by checking /var/log/dpkg.log

checks.name: Kernel is Current and Not Vulnerable
checks.purpose: Check that the running kernel is the latest available and no kernel security updates are pending in apt

remediation: Enable and configure Windows Update or a patch management solution (WSUS, SCCM, Intune). Establish a patching SLA: critical patches within 15-30 days, high within 30-90 days. On Linux, configure security repositories and schedule regular apt/yum update runs. Maintain logs of patch activity and remediate pending reboots promptly.
